title: Protein Carbonyl Assay Protocol
author: Peter Havel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.63hhgj6

steps.title: Transfer 200 µl of sample to...
steps.instruction: Transfer 200 µl of sample to two 2 ml plastic tubes. One tube will be the sample tube (S#) and the other will be the control tube (C#).

steps.title: Add 800 µl of DNPH to...
steps.instruction: Add 800 µl of DNPH to the sample tube and add 800 µl of 2.5 M HCI to the control tube.

steps.title: Incubate both tubes (S# & C#)...
steps.instruction: Incubate both tubes (S# & C#) in the dark at room temperature for one hour. Vortex each tube briefly every 15 minutes during the incubation.

steps.title: Add 1 ml of 20% TCA...
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml of 20% TCA to each tube and vortex. Place tubes on ice and incubate for five minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes at 10,000 xg for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes at 10,000 xg for 10 minutes at 4ºC in a microcentrifuge.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 ml of 10% TCA. Place tubes on ice and let sit for five minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes at 10,000 xg for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes at 10,000 xg for 10 minutes at 4ºC in a microcentrifuge.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 ml of (1:1) Ethanol/Ethyl Acetate mixture. Manually suspend pellet with spatula, vortex thoroughly, and centrifuge tubes at 10,000 xg for 10 minutes at 4ºC in a microcentrifuge.

steps.title: After the final wash, resuspend the...
steps.instruction: After the final wash, resuspend the protein pellets in 500µl of guanidine hydrochloride by vortexing.

steps.title: Centriguge tubes at 10,000 xg for...
steps.instruction: Centriguge tubes at 10,000 xg for 10 minutes at 4ºC in a microcentrifuge to remove any left over debris.

steps.title: Transfer 220 µl of suspernatant from...
steps.instruction: Transfer 220 µl of suspernatant from the sample (S#) tube to two wells of the 96-well plate.

steps.title: Transfer 220 µl of supernatant from...
steps.instruction: Transfer 220 µl of supernatant from the control (C#) tube to two wells of the 96-well plate.

steps.title: Measure the absorbance at a wavelength...
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance at a wavelength between 360-385 nm using a plate reader.

steps.title: Calculate the average absorbance of each sample and control
steps.instruction: Calculate the average absorbance of each sample and control.

steps.title: Subtract the average absorbance of the...
steps.instruction: Subtract the average absorbance of the controls from the average absorbance of the samples. This is the Corrected Absorbance (CA).

steps.title: Determine the concentration of the carbonyls...
steps.instruction: Determine the concentration of the carbonyls by inserting the corrected absorbance into the following equation: *The actual extinction coefficient for dinitrophenylhydrazine at 370 nm is 22,000 M -1 cm -1 (0.022 µM -1 cm -1 ). This value has been adjusted for the pathlength of the solution in the well.
